notes: Reproduced as reported: SKTypeface.FromFamilyName returns 'Noto Mono' regardless of requested font family in Blazor WASM. This is a WASM platform limitation — Skia's font manager in WASM only has access to fonts bundled into the binary (Noto Mono by default), not system or browser fonts. Cross-platform test on macOS console confirms FromFamilyName works correctly on native platforms. Workaround is to load fonts explicitly via SKTypeface.FromStream() or SKTypeface.FromData().

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WebAssembly project with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.2
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 3.119.2
reproductionSteps.output: Restore succeeded. PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor' version '3.119.2' added.

reproductionSteps.description: Add WasmBuildNative to csproj and create repro page with reporter's SKTypeface.FromFamilyName code
reproductionSteps.command: Edit Repro.csproj to add <WasmBuildNative>true</WasmBuildNative>, create Pages/Home.razor with SKTypeface.FromFamilyName('Tahoma', SKFontStyle.BoldItalic) and console output

filesCreated.description: Blazor page with SKCanvasView calling SKTypeface.FromFamilyName and logging the result to console

reproductionSteps.description: Build Blazor WASM project with native compilation
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded with 5 warning(s). Warnings are for obsolete SKPaint.TextSize, SKPaint.Typeface, and SKCanvas.DrawText overloads.

reproductionSteps.description: Run Blazor WASM app and verify SKTypeface.FromFamilyName behavior in browser
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet run --urls http://localhost:5112 (then navigate with Playwright browser)
reproductionSteps.output:
Browser console:
REPRO: Requested 'Tahoma', got 'Noto Mono'
REPRO: Match=False
SUCCESS: rendered 1600x1200

Screenshot confirms text rendered in Noto Mono monospace font, showing 'Requested: Tahoma' and 'Got: Noto Mono'.

reproductionSteps.description: Cross-platform test: console app on macOS with same FromFamilyName calls to verify WASM-specific behavior
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new console && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.119.2 && dotnet run
reproductionSteps.output:
Requested: 'Tahoma' => Got: 'Tahoma' Match=True
Requested: 'Arial' => Got: 'Arial' Match=True
Requested: 'Calibri' => Got: 'Helvetica' Match=False
Requested: 'Helvetica' => Got: 'Helvetica' Match=True
Requested: 'Times New Roman' => Got: 'Times New Roman' Match=True

versionResults.notes: FromFamilyName('Tahoma') returns 'Noto Mono' in Blazor WASM. All font family requests fall back to Noto Mono, the only bundled font.
Same behavior on main branch: FromFamilyName('Tahoma') returns 'Noto Mono' in Blazor WASM sample. Tested via samples/Basic/BlazorWebAssembly.
Console app on macOS: FromFamilyName correctly resolves Tahoma, Arial, Helvetica, Times New Roman. Only Calibri falls back (expected — macOS doesn't have Calibri).

corrections.corrected: Confirmed as by-design WASM limitation. Triage analysis was correct — this is expected platform behavior, not a SkiaSharp bug.

actionability.reason: WASM sandbox prevents access to system/browser fonts. Skia's font manager in WASM only has access to bundled fonts (Noto Mono by default). This is a fundamental platform limitation, not a SkiaSharp bug. Reporter and community have confirmed the FromStream/FromData workaround works.

workarounds: Use SKTypeface.FromStream() with an embedded resource font file (.ttf/.otf)
Use SKTypeface.FromData() with font data fetched via HttpClient
Register loaded typefaces as singleton services to avoid reloading on each paint call

actions.description: Close as by-design WASM platform limitation
